tasks.title: worx-claw exchange-api: Harden webhook delivery (concurrency cap, delivery log, HMAC timestamp)
tasks.description:
Security and reliability hardening for webhook delivery (exchange-api). Planner-approved revised plan from cycle-96 debate:
(1) webhookSubscriptions schema: id uuid PK, agentId FK, url varchar(500), secret varchar(255) PLAINTEXT (server-generated 32-byte hex, returned once only), events text[], active boolean, createdAt.
(2) webhookDeliveryLog schema: id uuid PK, webhookSubId FK ON DELETE CASCADE, eventType varchar(100), attempt smallint, statusCode smallint, success boolean, deliveredAt.
(3) Migration 0009: webhook_delivery_log table + idx_delivery_log_sub(webhook_sub_id, delivered_at DESC).
(4) webhookDelivery.ts: signPayload(secret,body,ts) returns Stripe-pattern t={ts},v1={hmac(ts.body)}; deliverWebhook logs each attempt to webhook_delivery_log (fire-and-forget insert); prom Counter exchange_webhook_delivery_total{success}.
(5) fireWebhookEvents: Promise pool with CONCURRENCY_CAP=10 using inFlight counter; release in finally{} to prevent slot leak.
(6) POST /api/v2/webhooks: secret=crypto.randomBytes(32).toString(hex) server-generated; return 201 {id,url,events,active,createdAt,secret}. GET omits secret. DELETE WHERE id AND agent_id → 404 if rowCount=0.
(7) 9 vitest tests: subscribe 201 with secret, GET omits secret, DELETE 204, unauthorized DELETE 404, signPayload format, deliverWebhook 3-retry, unauthorized (agent B cannot delete agent A sub), delivery failure logs success:false, HMAC verify.

tasks.title: exchange-api: Add Kubernetes NetworkPolicy manifests for zero-trust pod-level network security
tasks.description: The current K8s manifests have no network policies — any pod in the cluster can reach exchange-api, PostgreSQL, and Redis directly, bypassing the Ingress rate limiter. Fix with zero-trust pod-to-pod policies: (1) k8s/network-policies/deny-all-ingress.yaml — default deny all pod ingress for namespace exchange-api; (2) k8s/network-policies/allow-ingress-nginx.yaml — allow only pods with label app.kubernetes.io/name=ingress-nginx to reach exchange-api pods on port 3000; (3) k8s/network-policies/allow-exchange-to-postgres.yaml — allow exchange-api pods to reach PostgreSQL pods (label app=postgresql or externalIP) on port 5432; (4) k8s/network-policies/allow-exchange-to-redis.yaml — allow exchange-api pods to reach Redis pods on port 6379; (5) k8s/network-policies/allow-prometheus-scrape.yaml — allow pods with label app=prometheus to reach exchange-api on port 3000 for /metrics; (6) k8s/network-policies/allow-exchange-to-jaeger.yaml — allow exchange-api to reach Jaeger on port 14268 (traces); (7) k8s/network-policies/kustomization.yaml; (8) Update k8s/README.md with Network Policies section explaining label requirements; (9) 4 vitest tests: validate each NetworkPolicy YAML parses cleanly, verify correct podSelector labels match deployment labels, verify policyTypes contains Ingress or Egress, verify default-deny spec has empty podSelector. Impact: Without NetworkPolicy, a compromised pod can directly reach the database — this closes a critical lateral-movement attack vector required for SOC2 and production security compliance.

Impact: prevents fraudulent check-ins (workers checking in from home) which is a key trust and payment integrity issue in Korean gig market.

tasks.title: exchange-api: Add PostgreSQL row-level security for multi-tenant org data isolation
tasks.description: Harden multi-tenant data isolation by enforcing org_id scoping at the database layer: (1) Add org_id UUID column to agent_profiles, gig_agent_profiles, job_posts, api_keys (migration 0010); (2) Create PostgreSQL RLS policies: ALTER TABLE agent_profiles ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY; CREATE POLICY org_isolation ON agent_profiles USING (org_id = current_setting('app.org_id')::uuid); (3) Set session variable in Drizzle connection wrapper: db.execute(sql`SET LOCAL app.org_id = ${orgId}`) before all queries; (4) Add org_id to the requireAuth middleware - extract from api_key_orgs join; (5) Add migration rollback script if RLS breaks existing keyless dev workflow (FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY on dev=false); (6) Update drizzle schema to include org_id FK on all relevant tables; (7) Integration test: verify org A cannot read org B profiles via direct DB query or API; (8) 7 vitest tests: org isolation enforced, cross-org read blocked, dev mode bypass works, RLS policy exists check, migration rollback safe, admin bypass (superuser), API returns 403 on cross-org. Production blocker: currently org data isolation is purely application-layer - a SQL injection or middleware bug could leak cross-org data.

tasks.title: exchange-api: Fix SSE stream to deliver match.found events to connected agents
tasks.description:
Fix proactive match SSE delivery: sse.ts currently only listens for EVENT_LOGGED_EVENT on itEventStream; proactiveMatch.ts correctly emits match.found but that event name is never consumed by the SSE route handler.

Fix: (1) Add a separate listener on itEventStream for match.found events in sse.ts; (2) Filter by agentId (send only to the matching agent's SSE connection — currently SSE is per api_key, need to check if agentId maps to api_key); (3) Format as SSE data event with {event_type: match.found, agentId, jobPostId, score, timestamp}; (4) Add TODO note about Redis pub/sub needed for multi-instance deployments.

Alternatively: convert proactiveMatch emit to use EVENT_LOGGED_EVENT with event_type=match.found to reuse existing SSE pipeline.

1 vitest test: emit match.found on itEventStream → SSE client receives it.
tasks.result: PASS. sse.ts: match.found listener with agentId filtering + cleanup. 2 vitest tests. PR #94 merged.

tasks.title: AlbaConnect: Add production deployment to Fly.io with managed PostgreSQL and Redis
tasks.description: Deploy AlbaConnect to production on Fly.io for real-world validation. TOSS_SECRET_KEY=... etc.; (7) Add fly.json health probe config pointing to /health endpoint; (8) Test deploy via fly deploy --local-only --dry-run to validate config. tasks.title: exchange-api: Add recruiter match-history and saved-searches for workflow continuity
tasks.description: Recruiters need persistent workflow state across sessions: (1) Table: search_sessions(id UUID PK, api_key_id UUID FK, query TEXT, filters JSONB, result_count INT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ) — logs each search invocation; (2) Table: saved_searches(id UUID PK, api_key_id UUID FK, name VARCHAR(100), query TEXT, filters JSONB, last_run_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ); (3) Table: profile_interactions(id UUID PK, api_key_id UUID FK, profile_id UUID FK, interaction_type ENUM(viewed|contacted|shortlisted|rejected), notes TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, UNIQUE(api_key_id, profile_id, interaction_type)); (4) POST /api/v2/searches/saved {name, query, filters} — save a search config; GET /api/v2/searches/saved — list (limit 50); DELETE /api/v2/searches/saved/:id; POST /api/v2/searches/saved/:id/run — re-execute saved search; (5) POST /api/v2/profiles/:id/interact {type, notes?} — record interaction; GET /api/v2/profiles/:id/interactions — list interactions; (6) GET /api/v2/history — recent search history (last 20 sessions); (7) Drizzle migrations for all 3 tables; (8) 5 vitest tests: save search, run saved search returns results, duplicate shortlist upserts, interaction history shape, history pagination. Impact: recruiters using exchange-api must rebuild search context every session — saved searches and interaction history enable stateful multi-session workflows.
tasks.result: PASS (orchestrator direct, stale-lock recovery). 5 files +713 lines. search_sessions+saved_searches+profile_interactions tables (migration 0025), POST/GET/DELETE /searches/saved, POST /searches/saved/:id/run (session log+last_run_at), GET /history (paginated), POST/GET /profiles/:id/interact(ions). 5/5 new vitest, tsc clean, 433/433 existing unaffected. PR#96 merged.

tasks.title: exchange-api: Add agent portfolio and CV attachment support with presigned URL storage
tasks.description: Allow agents to attach CV and portfolio documents to their profiles: (1) Table: profile_attachments(id UUID PK, profile_id UUID FK agent_profiles, gig_profile_id UUID FK gig_agent_profiles NULLABLE, attachment_type ENUM(cv|portfolio|certificate|sample_work), url TEXT NOT NULL, filename VARCHAR(255), size_bytes INT, content_type VARCHAR(100), is_public BOOL DEFAULT true, uploaded_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(), UNIQUE(profile_id, attachment_type) where type=cv); (2) POST /api/v2/profiles/:id/attachments — register attachment metadata (URL is client-provided presigned S3 URL or external link); requires write:profiles scope; validate URL format; max 10 attachments per profile; (3) GET /api/v2/profiles/:id/attachments — list attachments (public if is_public=true, otherwise requires auth); include in search result rows as has_cv, attachment_count; (4) DELETE /api/v2/profiles/:id/attachments/:attachmentId — requires write:profiles scope and profile ownership; (5) Completeness scoring update: cv attachment present adds +10pts to completeness score; (6) Search result enrichment: add has_cv:bool, attachment_count:int to search result rows; (7) Migration for profile_attachments table; (8) 5 vitest tests: attach CV, list public attachments, non-owner cannot delete, CV adds completeness points, search result includes has_cv. Impact: exchange-api profiles have no way to attach CVs or portfolios — this closes a critical gap for real-world recruiter workflows where CV review is the primary qualifying step.
